def: n. f. S. अहो Pr. avvo . A sigh; deep sigh; suspiration. Tulsī, āh garīb kī Hari sūṅ sahī na jāë, Muī khāl kī phūṅk se loh bhasam ho jāë . Tulsī Dās. Hari the sighs deep of the poor cannot endure, The bellow's breath doth ashes make of iron sure. āh paṛnā , v. n. To be hit by another's sighs; to pay the penalty of the injuries one has done. āh karnā, bharnā, kheṅchnā, mārnā, yā nikālnā , v. n. To sigh; give, fetch, or heave a sigh. Āh karūṅ to jag jale, jaṅgal bhī jal jāë, Pāpī jiyarā nā jale, jis meṅ āh samāë . Dohā. My sighs might burn up all the world, and e'en the forest too, But yet this sinful heart wont burn from whence these sighs issue. āh-i-sard ; H. ṭhanḍā sāṅs , n. f. A deep sigh; a long deep sigh.
more: intj. Oh! ah! alas! alack! ah me; woe is me! 1. Āh! daī kaisī bhaī, an-chāhat ke saṅg, Dīpak ke bhāveṅ nahīṅ, jal jal mare pataṅg . Dohā. Ah me! what's this? My heart giv'n one who loves me not, The moth that burns and dies the flame regards not aught. 2. Aise zālim ko dil diyā ham ne, Āh Āllā! ye kyā kīyā ham ne! Raṅgīn.

1. s. f. A sigh. 2. interj. Alas! ah! آه بهرنا āh bharnā or آه کرنا āh karnā, or آه مارنا āh mārnā, To sigh. آه جانکاه āh-i-jān-kāh, A heart-rending sigh. آه جگرسوز āh-i-jigar-soz, A heart-burning sigh.
wety: Onomatopoeic.

def: intj. Ah! alas! oh dear! heigho! (exclamation of pain, pity, &c.); — Ha! O! holloa! bravo! capital! how excellent! well done! strange! wonderful! how extraordinary!

intj. S. अहह. 1. An exclamation of pleasure, as O! Ah! 1. Ajab ālam hai mastī kā, ahā-hā-hā! oho-ho-ho! Maë o sāqī haiṅ sab ek jā, ahā-hā-hā? oho-ho-ho! Zafar. Intoxicating joy, most exquisite excess! Ahā-hā-hā! Oho-ho-ho! Woman and wine their warm solicitations press. Ahā-hā-hā! Oho-ho-ho! 2. Shab ko huīṅ ahā-hā-ha! zor mazoṅ kī mastiyāṅ, Bijlī kī shakleṅ bantiyāṅ, būṅdeṅ paṛī barastiyāṅ . Nazīr. Last night, ahā-hā-hā! enjoyment keen and transports wild, The lightning's varied flashes bright, the rain drops dropping mild. 2. Hallo! lo! Āhā! tum ā gaë . 3. Bravo! well done! ( khūb ); hear hear! Āhā! khūb kīyā! 4. Good gracious; good heavens! 5. Heigh ho! well a day! 6. Hurra! huzza! Kuchh saineṅ tirchhī sehr-bharī, kuchh ghāt lagāvaṭ khū kī hai, Kuchh shor ahā-hā-hā! kā hai, kuchh dhūm oho-ho-ho! kī hai . Holī. Nazīr. Now witching sidelong glances cast, Now meetings planned, attachments fast, Now shout hurras—ahā-hā-hā! Now swell huzzas—oho-ho-ho!

def: s.m. A body of Indian veterans of the time of the Emperor Akbar, somewhat of the nature of pensioners, but liable to be called out for active service on emergency; (hence) one who is supported by the king as a pensioner, without rendering service of any kind; (hence) a lazy, indolent fellow.
more: n. m. 1. One who is supported by the king at his ease, rendering no service and not taking part in any active occupation. 2. A lazy, indolent fellow who will not work; also called an ahdī bail (an ox too tired or sick to stir or get up, and performing the functions of nature where he lies); a drone; sluggard. Ahdī paṛā hī rahegā, uṭṭhegā bhī? Wom. 3. An Indian military corps in the reign of Akbar, so named because it was called out only on great emergencies, in consideration of important victories obtained by the corps. ahdī banā'nā,—nikāl'nā , v. a. To exhibit an ahdī (as is done in the Holī).

r: ʻain, ʻain-ě-mǒhmala, ʻain-ě-gair-manqūt̤a, alif, laʻl, lāl, hamza
def: (called ʻain-ě-mǒhmala, or ʻain-ě-gair-manqūt̤a), is the twenty-fourth letter of the Urdū alphabet (the eighteenth of the Arabic). This letter is peculiarly Arabic or Semitic, and occurs only in such Hindūstānī words as are borrowed from the Arabic, and in the secondary formations from these; or in the arabicized form of Persian words in which it has replaced an original alif (e.g. لعل laʻl, for لال lāl). It is a strong (but, to Europeans and most other people, unpronounceable) guttural, related in its nature to the hamza, and ḥ; and is described as produced by a smart compression of the fauces and forcible emission of breath. By the common people of India it is sounded like alif. As a numeral it stands for 70.
more: called عين مـﮩمله or عين غيرمنقوطه the 18th letter of the Arabic alphabet, does not occur in pure Persian and has no analogous character in Sanskrit. It is one of the radical letters. In abjad it stands for 70.

the eighteenth letter of the Arabic alphabet, does not occur in pure Persian, and has no analagous character in Sanskrit. It is one of the guttural letters, being formed in the lower part of the throat, by a compression of the fauces stronger and deeper than that used in the formation of hamza. Its sound has been compared to the voice of a calf calling its mother, or to that of a person who is making some painful exertion. It is one of the radical letters. In the denotation of numbers by the Arabic letters, it stands for 70. In Persian words introduced into Arabic it is substituted for ا as لعل for لال.

def: v.t. To draw, pull, drag, attract; to string up, hang (usually aiṅć-denā); to draw or rule (a line, &c.); to write, scribble; to sketch; to keep or hold back or aloof
more: v. a. S. इष् to try to obtain. Caus. of iṅch'nā , q. v. 1. To draw a line; to rule. Lakīr (yā ḍaṅḍīr) eṅch do . 2. To write; scribble. Do harf eṅch do . 3. To draw or unsheath (one's sword). Miyān se eṅch lī . 4. To hang (on a gibbet) ( phāṅsī denā ); execute. Gadar meṅ saikṛoṅ ko eṅch dīyā . 5. To take in; absorb; suck up. Chūnā sārī chiknāī eṅch legā . 6. To draw in the breath; inspire; inhale. Do dam eṅch lūṅ, to chalūṅ 7. To extort; exact; ease of (one's (money. Aṅgrez sārā rupaya eṅch le gaë . The English have carried away all the money from the country. 8. To reserve; keep or hold back. 9. To take upon oneself; engage; make oneself liable to pay for; to be responsible for. Banye ne rupayā īṅch līyā .
v. a. To draw, to attract, to pull.
subs: adv. With difficulty, by hook or by crook
v.t. To draw out; draw up, draw in, imbibe, absorb, suck up; to take, take in; to take away by force, carry off (usually eṅć-le-jānā), wring from, extort.

def: adj. 1. Useless; worthless; unserviceable; unprofitable. 2. Incomplete; unfinished; imperfect. aiṅḍ kar'nā , v. n. To render useless; to break; injure; spoil. aiṅḍ honā, aiṅḍ rah'nā , v. n. 1. To be broken, dislocated, injured. 2. To come to a dead lock or stand-still (a machine). Kal aiṅḍ ho gaī . 3. To be locked up; remain uninvested (as money). Sau rupaë aiṅḍ ho gaë . 4. To become useless, or good for nothing. Tum to bilkul aiṅḍ ho gaë . 5. To remain unfinished, incomplete, or in statu quo. Tumhāre bagair sārā kām aiṅḍ ho gayā .
adj. Rolling; lolling; idle, useless, unserviceable, worthless; incomplete, unfinished
subs: v.t. To render useless or unserviceable; to injure; to break
v.n. To become useless, worthless, unserviceable, &c., to get out of order, be injured, broken, spoiled; to be unfinished or incomplete.

def: s.m. A make-weight (placed in the lighter scale).
n. m. A makeweight placed in the lighter scale. W.
adj. (f. -ī), Staggering, rolling, reeling, swaggering, mincing; crooked, zigzag; harsh, unseemly, rude, coarse (speech, &c.)
subs: v.t. To call names, to abuse, villify.

def: = H اينڐانا ऐंडाना aiṅḍānā, (caus. of aiṅḍnā, q.v.) To cause to roll or sway from side to side, &c.; to put in the scale, to weigh roughly.
v. a. To put in the scale; to weigh roughly.
= H اينڐالنا ऐंडालना aiṅḍālnā, (caus. of aiṅḍnā, q.v.) To cause to roll or sway from side to side, &c.; to put in the scale, to weigh roughly.

adj. 1. Crooked; oblique; zigzag; serpentine. 2. Harsh; rough; rude; unpolite ( aukhī ). aiṅḍī baiṅḍī sunānā , v. a. To abuse; call names. aiṅḍī baiṅḍī chāl, mastānah chāl , n. f. A reeling motion; mincing gait; wanton air. Maiṅ Khatraiṅḍī, ishq lapeṭī, joban sāḍa bālā; Chāl chalūṅ koī aiṅḍī baiṅḍī, mārūṅ zulfoṅ-wālā . Panj. Chhallā . A love-souled Khatrānī am I, my charms scarce rounded quite, With mincing gait I'll pace along, some raven locks I'll smite.
v. n. To lie idle; roll in bed; loll; do nothing; fold one's arms. Phr. Not to move finger or foot. Khānā, aur aiṅḍnā! Prov. Saying. Eaten, and laid down!
v.n. To strut, stalk, swagger ( = aiṅṭhnā); to roll or turn from side to side, to roll about (as in bed); to stagger or reel (like a drunkard); to lie idle, loll about.
more: v. n. To strut, to stalk.

def: adj. Familiar with or relating to magic, magical, deceptive, illusory; — s.m. Magician, juggler.
adv. Here, hither.
adj. Of or relating to the eye; eye- (in comp., e.g. gawāh-ě-ʻainī, 'eye-witness'); — having a blemish in the eye, rejected (as a horse).
n. m. Civil law.
n. m. Criminal law.
n. m. Revenue law.

ety: for A. عينية, fem. fr. عيني ʻainī
def: adj. Of or relating to the eye or eyes.

ety: S. आवृत्त+कः, or from aiṅṭnā, q.v.
def: s.m. Twist, turn, convolution ( = aiṅṭan, aiṅṭhan, aiṅṭh), clew, hank, skein, &c. (syn. kalāba); the string of a spinning wheel on which the spindle works, cross-strap round wheels; — a snail.

s.f. = aiṅṭā, aiṅth, aiṅṭhan, q.q.v. s.m. Twist, turn, convolution ( = aiṅṭan, aiṅṭhan, aiṅṭh), clew, hank, skein, &c. (syn. kalāba); the string of a spinning wheel on which the spindle works, cross-strap round wheels; — a snail.
s.f. Twist, turn, convolution, coil, fold, ply; tightening; stiffness, rigidity; obstinacy, stubbornness; pride, arrogance; affectation, airs, strut (syn. akaṛ, q.v.)
s. f. Tightening, twist; strut.
n. f. See اکر Twist; turn; convolution.

s.f. Twist, &c. ( = aiṅṭh, q.v.); constipation; gripes, colic. s.f. Twist, turn, convolution, coil, fold, ply; tightening; stiffness, rigidity; obstinacy, stubbornness; pride, arrogance; affectation, airs, strut (syn. akaṛ, q.v.)
n. f. Costiveness; gripes; colic.
v.t. To twist, turn, wring; to cause to writhe; to cause to strut, &c.
more: v. a. (caus. of اينٿهنا) To twist, &c.

def: See akaṛ-bāz . aiṅṭh-kar chalnā , v. n. See akaṛ-kar chalnā .

v. a. 1. To twist; wind; spin. Tāge aiṅṭhnā . 2. To distort; make awry or crooked. 3. To squeeze; press; contract. Chhātī aiṅṭhnā . 4. ( ameṭhnā ). To chastise; punish. Kān aiṅṭhnā . 5. To tighten; make tight or fast. 6. To wrench; extort; take by force or fraud. Tum ne muft meṅ mere dām aiṅṭh līye . 7. To appropriate wrongfully; embezzle.
v. n. See اکرَنا Aiṅṭhtā kyā hai, tū sarīr ko dekh, Apnī paidāyash aur khamīr ko dekh . You turn and twist as on your form you gaze, Think whence you came, the leaven that did you raise.

v.t. To twist, wind, spin, make into hanks or skeins; to make crooked, to distort; to wring (the ear); to punish, chastise; to tighten, squeeze, press, contract; to wrench from, extort, take by force or fraud, appropriate wrongfully; — v.n. To twist, writhe, wriggle; to cramp; to be tightened, cramped, pressed, to shrink, be contracted; to become rigid, stiff, &c.; to strut, stalk, &c. ( = akaṛnā, q.v.)
more: v. a. To tighten. v. n. To writhe, to cramp, to twist; to strut, to stalk, to walk affectedly.
subs: adv. Stiffly, rigidly; affectedly, with a strut, proudly, &c. ( = akaṛ-kar)
v.t. (intens. of aiṅṭhnā), To twist tight, &c.; to wring from, extort, &c.: see aiṅṭhnā.

def: (dakh.) Beneath, &c.: see پاٴين pāʼīn.
v.t. & n. To twist, &c. = aiṅṭhnā (the more common form).
more: v. n. (dakh. i. q. کشيده هونا) To stalk, to strut (?).

def: s.f. Petulance, peevishness; pride.
s.m. Proud, &c. = aiṅṭhū, akṛū, akaṛ-bāz, q.q.v.
s.m. Pride; affectation, &c. = akaṛ, q.v. s.m. (in Gram.) The letter अ a.

ety: Pehl. drafsh; Zend drafsha; S. drapsa
def: s.m.(?), A banner, ensign, standard; — a sash wound round the head or the helmet when going to battle; — light, splendour; lightning; anything glittering, shining, or flashing; — a cobbler's awl ( = darosh, q.v.).
more: s. 1. An instrument of shoemakers, &c., an awl. 2. A flag or banner raised on the day of battle. 3. Lightning: splendour, any thing splendid or shining. 4. A sash which on the day of battle they wind round the turband and helmet.
wety: From Classical Persian درفش (dirafš, “standard; banner”).

def: s.m. This one and that one; anyone and everyone (without distinction); stranger; a nobody-knows-who.
more: n. m. H. aur another, A. gair foreign. 1. This one and that one; every body; etc. Airī gairī fasal bauhterī . E. Prov. I shall find a mouthful somewhere or other. 2. A stranger; foreigner; nobody knows who.

def: s.m. Indra's elephant.
s.f. Name of the river Rāvī (in the Panjāb).

adj. & adv. (f. -ī), This-like, such, of this sort; like ( = sā, in which case it is subjoined to the accusatival base of a pronoun, and to the acc. or gen. case of a substantive, e.g. āp-aisā 'aqlmand, 'a wise man like you'; mard-aisā, 'like a man'; par-kī aisī lakīroṅ ke rūp, 'in the form of feathery streaks'); in such a manner, in this wise, thus, so; after this sort, of a piece; — as though, as if; — so much, this much ( = itnā)
more: adj. S. ईदृश, Pr. eriso , Pālī. ediso such; H. is sā like this. 1. Like; similar; such; resembling; twin ( joṛā ). 1. Aise ādmī kam hote haiṅ . 2. Khanjadyā kī māṅ masurī kī dāl as pakāwaīt rahe, ki murgī ke suruā ke māt karaīt rahei . Bhoj. Prov. Khanjadyā's mother used to prepare such a delicious mess of masūr dāl (pulse) that chicken soup was nothing to it. (Said of his late wife to his new spouse who is a bad hand at cooking). 2. Matching; corresponding; analogous; parallel. 3. Of this kind or sort; of a piece. Khānā jahāṅ thā baṭṭā, wāṅ jā-ke dhakke khāë. Kambakht ko jo khānā aksar milā to aisā . On Poverty. Nazīr. And should he go where bread is served out free, he is pushed out, A dinner served out to the poor is oft a serving out.

adv. 1. After this manner; in this way; so; as. 1. Bandī jab shādī kartī hai, aisī hī kartī hai . Wom. Prov. Saying. When I marry my children it is always so (a grand affair)! 2. Aise gaë, jaise gadhe ke sir se sīṅg . Prov. Clean gone as horns from the head of a donkey. 3. Aisā gayā, jaise mahfil meṅ se jūtā . Prov. Vanished as shoes from an assembly. 2. As though; as if; belike; likely. 3. So much; this much; to such an extent (itnā).

(from اس This, and سا Like, or for Sans. ईदृश Like this) adj. Such, this-like, so: resembling, like (particularly when joined to other words; as, مردايسا mard aisā, Like a man). ايساتيسا aisā taisā, or ايساويسا aisā waisā, So so, indifferent, indifferently.
subs: adj. Such and such, so-so, &c. = aisā-waisā, q.v.; — s.m. (f. aisī-taisī), Such an one, a nondescript, disreputable person; one destitute of discrimination, or of carefulness

adj. & adv. (f. aisī-waisī), So-so, passable, middling; indifferent, of little moment or worth, insignificant, inferior, poor, bad; improper, indecent (act or language); — indifferently, passably, &c. ( = aise waise)
adv. Thus, so, &c. = aisā; — also = aise-hī, q.v.
aise, intj. (colloq.) Really! is it so! surely not! you don't say so! what next!
s.f. Disgrace, infamy, ignominy; a polite term for anything unmentionable
adj. Just so, precisely; — as if, as though, so to speak; — accidentally, casually; without motive or reason ( = yūṅ-hī).

def: adj. Of little moment, middling.

def: n. m. aisī-taisī , n. f. S. तादृस्, Pr. tāriso . Such a one; a bad lot; a shady card; one who is no better than she should be; a scoundrel; one accursed. 1. Ye to jaldī se kahīṅ, yā Rab, mare, Aisā-taisā ho, jo byāh ab phir kare! Saudā. O God! may this one only quickly die somehow! May I be such a one if 'gain I wed, I vow! 2. Koī aisā-taisā yāṅ āwe to sahī! Dare such a scoundrel come here! aisā na ho, Khudā na kare ; P. mabādā, Khudā na khāstah , intj. Lest; may it not be; Heaven preserve us; God forbid.

adj. 1. Passable; indifferent; so so; middling; mediocre . 2. Trifling; trivial; insignificant; of little moment or worth. Maiṅ kyā aisā-waisā hūṅ jo dabūṅ . 3. Inferior; below par; paltry; petty. 4. Miserable; wretched; faint; feeble. 5. Foul; abusive; bad; obscene; indecent. Aisī waisī bāt mat kaho . 6. Evil; wrong; ill. Aisī-waisī bāt ho jāëgī, to tumhāre sir paṛegī. aisā-waisā honā, yūṅ tūṅ honā , v. n. (Wom). To suffer injury; to die. (A curse). Tumhāre ghar-ke sab aise waise ho jāëṅ! Wom.
adv. And so forth; etcetera. aise aise , adj. Such like; such as; similar. Aise aisoṅ ko maiṅ samajhtā kyā hūṅ!
(dakh.) In the mean time.

adv. Perhaps ( shāid ); as if; as much as to say. aise meṅ, itne meṅ , adv. 1. In the meantime; while. 2. Now; this time ( ab ). Aise meṅ khulā hai, chale jāo. aise hī , adv. Incidentally; casually; accidentally ( yūṅ hī ). aise hī hai , adv. Exactly; no doubt ( yūṅ hī hai ).

def: (see ايسا) adv. Thus.

ety: inf. n. of عيش 'to live'; 'to live a pleasant life'
def: s.m. (orig.) 'Life; animal life'; a life of pleasure and enjoyment, pleasure, delight, luxury; gratification of the appetites, sensuality; carnal intercourse
more: n. m. 1. Luxury; a bed of roses; a bed of down ( ashrat ). 2. Animal gratification; sexual intercourse. aish uṛānā, yā k. , v. n. 1. To enjoy oneself. 2. To riot and revel in sensual enjoyments. aish ke bande, aishī-bande . Sensualists; Sybarites. aish mahal , n. m. The inner or female apartments of the house of a person of wealth or rank. aish o ashrat, aish o nishāt , n. Sensual pleasures; voluptuousness; debauch; orgies ( raṅg ralyāṅ ). aish, yā aish o ashrat se guzarnā , v. n. To live well; pass one's days in luxurious enjoyment; enjoy oneself.
s. m. Pleasure, delight. عيش گاه aﻌish-gāh, s. f. Place of merriment. عيش محل aﻌish-maḥall, Banqueting-house.
subs: or ʻaish karnā, v.n. To enjoy oneself; to revel in pleasure, or in sensual enjoyments
s.m. The slave of pleasure; a sensualist, a sybarite
s.f. or ʻaish-maḥal, s.m. Place or house of enjoyment or pleasure; — ʻaish-maḥal, Banqueting-house; — the inner or women's apartments of the house of a person of wealth or rank
s.m. Joy and delight, pleasure, merriment, excessive pleasure
s.f. or ʻaish-o-nashāt̤, s.m. Pleasantness of life; luxurious enjoyment; sensual pleasures, voluptuousness, &c.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian عَیْش (ayš), borrowed from Arabic عَيْش (ʕayš), from عَاشَ (ʕāša).

def: adj. Made of reeds.
n. f. Grandeur; wealth; glory; prosperity. īsh-varī , n. f. An epithet of Durgā, Lakshmī, or any other of the Shaktis or female energies.

intj. Really; you dont say so; what next; only think; who'd have thought it! Ek mast ko jo kheṅchā zāhid ne, to woh bolā, "Aisī jī! milk ṭhairī, goyā kisī kī masjid? Inshā. A drunkard from a masjid was pulled out, says he, "Heyday! the house of God a private property!"

n. f. 1. Disgrace; dishonor. Āh re dil ye tū ne kaisī kī, Hat tere dil kī aisī taisī kī! Miskīṅ. 2. A polite phrase for the anus . Apnī aisī taisī marā-ke wahāṅ phir kyoṅ gaë? aisī taisī meṅ jā'nā , v. n. To go to pot, blazes, h—, etc. Bārah gāṅo kā chaudhrī, assī gāṅo kā rāo, Apne kām na āwe, to aisī taisī meṅ jāo . Prov. A squire of twice six villages, a lord of four score, he May go to h—for me if he's no benefit to me.
adv. This year.

s.m. Control, dominion, sway, supremacy, power, might; grandeur, glory, pomp, wealth, prosperity, greatness, majesty, state; superhuman power, omnipotence (of the Deity); the divine faculties of omnipresence, invisibility, &c.
adj. Endowed with superhuman qualities; grand, glorious, majestic, &c.

v.n. = aiṅṭhnā, q.v. v.t. To twist, writhe, wriggle; to cramp; to be tightened, cramped, pressed, to shrink, be contracted; to become rigid, stiff, &c.; to strut, stalk, &c. ( = akaṛnā, q.v.)

v.t. (intens. of aiṅṭhnā), To twist tight, &c.; to wring from, extort, &c.: see aiṅṭhnā.

def: intj. See s.v. ai. s.m. The letter or sound ai.
s.m. Hall, gallery, portico (applied generally to a royal palace).
more: s. m. A hall, a gallery, an open hall supported on columns.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اَیْوَان (aywān).

ety: from ewaṛ and wārā = S. wāṭakah
def: s.m. A shed in a jungle for goats, sheep, or cattle.
more: n. A cowshed in the middle of a jungle.

def: adj. Having the menses ( = kapṛon-se).
more: n. m. pl. of يوم a day H. din . 1. Time ( waqt ); space; days; season. 2. Duration; period; term; usance. 3. The menses; monthly discharge. aīyām ohdeh-dārī , n. m. Incumbency; the period during which an office is held. aīyām guzashtah , n. m. Time past. aīyām se, kapṛoṅ se, maile sir se, yā nahānī honā, phūl ānā , v. n. Wom. To have the menses or monthly discharge.
s. m. (plur. of يوم Heb. ) 1. Days, times, time. 2. Season, weather.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic أَيَّام (ʔayyām).

def: adj. Of time; for a long time; durable.

ety: intens. n. fr. عير 'to move to and fro'
def: adj. & s.m. Sharp, artful, shrewd, cunning, sly, mischievous (syn. ćatur); — an artful or crafty fellow, a knave.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian عیار (iyār), from Arabic عِيَار (ʕiyār).

def: s.f. An artful woman, a sly or cunning jade.
more: (from عير) adj. f. A sly, artful, or cunning woman.

def: s.f. = H عيارپن ʻaiyār-pan, s.m. = H عيارپنا ʻaiyār-panā, (abst. s. fr. ʻaiyār) = ʻaiyārī, q.v.
more: s. (dakh.) See عيارگي aﻌiyār-gī and عياري aﻌiyar-ī.

ety: ʻaiyār+ī = Zend aff. i = S. इ
def: s.f. Artfulness, slyness, cunning, craftiness, knavery, imposture; — (in Urdū) an artful woman ( = ʻaiyārnī, or ʻaiyāra).
more: n. f. Imposture; deception; circumvention ( chālākī , 4).

def: s.f. An artful woman, a sly or cunning jade.
s.m. = H عيارپنا ʻaiyār-panā, = P عيارگي ʻaiyāragī, s.f. (abst. s. fr. ʻaiyār) = ʻaiyārī, q.v.
= H عيارپن ʻaiyār-pan, s.m. = P عيارگي ʻaiyāragī, s.f. (abst. s. fr. ʻaiyār) = ʻaiyārī, q.v.

ety: intens. n. fr. عيش 'living,' inf. n. of عيش
def: adj. & s.m. Living well, luxurious, jovial, voluptuous, addicted to pleasure; libidinous, lecherous; — one who is addicted to pleasure, a voluptuary, sensualist, debauchee, rake.
more: adj. عيش he lived. 1. Luxurious; voluptuous. 2. Libidinous; lecherous; rakish. aiyāsh , n. m. A rake; a Don Juan; a debauchee.

ety: ʻaiyāsh, q.v.+ī = Zend aff. i = S. इ
def: s.f. Luxury, luxuriousness, joviality; the being addicted to pleasure, voluptuousness, sensuality, debauchery.
more: n. f. 1. Luxury; luxuriousness. 2. Animal gratification ( aubāshī , 1, 3).

def: s.m. Quantity; number; value.
prop. n. Job.

def: s.m. The star Capella, the bright star (a) upon the left shoulder of Auriga.

adv. Again, also, as before, item.

def: s. m. A he goat.

ety: v.n. fr. عوج 'to be crooked or curved,' &c.
def: s.m. Ivory.

adv. 1. To-day or to-morrow; in a few days; soon; "to-morrow and to-morrow". 1. Jān nā khāo, āj kal meṅ de dūṅgā . 2. Achchhā nahīṅ hai Mīr kā ahwāl in dinoṅ, Gālib ki ho chukegā yeh līmār āj kal . Mir. Mir's state of health in these days is not good at all—snuffed out. This sick man's like to be, to-morrow or to-day about. 3. Horī kā mahīno. saiyāṅ, der mat karyo, Mo ko āj kal sab maṅgb/?/ë de re; Mo ko chuṛyo kā j/?/ṛa, saīyāṅ lāë dere . Rus. Song. It is the month of Holī , husband, be not late, Get all things for me for these festive days, A set of bangles bring me, husband mine. 2. In these days; now-a-days; modern times. Āj kal bhāo charhā huā hai . 3. Of late; recently. Āj kal hī kā zikr hai. āj kal kar'nā, āj kal batā'nā , v. n. 1. To procrastinate; put off from day to day; delay; dilly dally; dally with. 2. To shilly shally; evade; shuffle. Woh taqāzā karte haiṅ, aur maiṅ āj kal kar rahā hāṅ . Gālib. āj kal ke,—kā,—kī , adj. Of the present day, age, or generation. Āj kal ke laṛke to apne muṅh se kahte haiṅ "hamārā byāh kar do." Kahānī .

adv. 1. Hitherto; to this day; as yet; till now; up to this time. 1. Sab ko neotal, keu na byāhal, Aja-hūṅ ādh kuṅār . Kabīr. She calleth all, but weddeth none. A virgin from the first she keeps. (Said of māyā , or the illusions of this world). 2. Āj-hūṅ lag mukh dikhātā naīn apas kā, Sajan mujh hāl suṅ kyā be-khabar hai! Walī. Till now he has not shewn his face to me, How heedless of my state my loved one seems! 2. Even now; just now; immediately; this very day; even to-day. āj fajar , adv. This morning. āj kidhar kā chāṅd hai? lit . On which side is the moon? Wonder of wonders! what a wonder! Āj kidhar kā chāṅd hai, jo tum idhar ā nīkle?
adv. To-day, this day; this time, now
more: adv. S. अद्य Pr. and Pālī. ajja . To-day; this day. 1. Āj nahīṅ, kal . Prov. Not to-day, to-morrow. This proverb is founded on the story of an ultra-pious Mahomedan who used every night to pray under a tree that God in his love would draw him to himself (Khudā apni muhabbat meṅ īṅch) . Prov. Saying. One night a wag, sitting on a branch overhead, let down a noose with which he began to haul up the devotee. "Not to-day", roared the pious man, "to-morrow." 2. Āj mare, kal dūsrā din . Prov. Dead to-day, to-morrow is but another day.

adv. To day. آج کل āj-kal, Nowadays, to day or to morrow, of late, in a few days. آج کل کرنا āj-kal karnā or آج کل بتانا āj-kal batānā, To procrastinate. آج کي دن āj ke din, To day. آج کي رات āj kī rāt, To night. آج کي روز āj ke roz, To day.
subs: adv. Up to this day; to this time, till now; hitherto; as yet
adv. To-day by day, to day
adj. (f. -ī), To-day's, this, of the present; new, recent, fresh; — āj-kī rāt, This night, to-night
āj-ke roz, adv. To-day — āj-kal, adv. To-day or to-morrow; in a few days, in a day or two, soon; in these days, now-a-days; recently, of late
āj-kal karnā, v.n. To put off from day to day, to procrastinate, delay; to shilly shally, evade, shuffle
adj. (f. -ī), Of the present day, age or generation; recent, fresh
ajahūṅ, adv. (emphatic), 1˚ = āj-tak, q.v. 2˚. This very day, to-day even; just now, even now, at this very time; immediately.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀅𑀚𑁆𑀚 (ajja), from Sanskrit अद्य (adyá).

def: s.f. A she-goat; the unreality of the universe, illusion (an epithet of Māyā, considered as the wife of the Supreme Being).
s.m. (f. -ī), Paternal grandfather
more: E. n. m. S. आर्य्यक् grandfather. A paternal grandfather (dādā) .

n. f. 1. A she-goat. Pakhā bīch daī pī gaë, bīte 1 bāj-ahār . 2 Ajā-bhaksh bhejo nahī, 3 duā-das karūṅ ahār . Enigma. My husband pledged his word in two weeks he'd be back—months have gone by— Not ev'n a letter he has sent to me—I'll poison take and die. 2. Illusion; the illusive universe; an epithet of Māyā considered as the wife of the Supreme Being.
subs: s.m. A guru's guru, a grandguru.

def: s.m. Wonder; astonishment; admiration; a wonderful thing; — adj. Wonderful, marvellous, astonishing, amazing, miraculous, strange, extraordinary, rare; droll, &c.
more: adj. عجب wondered. Astonishing ( ajgut ). 1. Tum bhī ajab log ho! A nice fellow you are! 2. Un se kuchh ajab nahīṅ hai. ujūba , n. m. A wonderful thing; a miracle.

(Heb. ) s. m. Wonder, admiration, astonishment; adj. Wonderful, astonishing, amazing, miraculous, rare, strange, droll, &c. ¶ uﻌjb, s. m. Pride, haughtiness. عجب کرنا aﻌjab karnā, To be astonished, to wonder.
subs: v.n. To wonder, to be astonished, &c.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian عَجَب (ajab), from Arabic عَجَب (ʕajab).

def: = اجہون ajahūṅ, q.v.s.v. آج āj. v.s.v. آج āj.
more: (آج and هو) adv. To day too, even now, just now, yet.

def: v.s.v. آج āj.
more: (آج and هون) adv. See اجهو aj-hū.

subs: s.m. The wonders of the creation; monsters

def: part. adj. Overtaken by fate, doomed; on the point of death; wretched, unfortunate
more: n. f. اجل the appointed term. 1. Death (maut) ; a term or appointed time (kāl) ; the hour of death; natural death. 2. The term of life; fate; destiny; hand of destiny (Karam) . 3. Met . A creditor who demands the payment of a debt at the appointed time. ajal rasīdah,—giriftah, shāmat kā mārā , adj. 1. Overtaken by fate; doomed; on the point of death. 2. Wretched; unfortunate. ajal-giriftah , A. P. n. A fore-doomed victim or sacrifice.

s. f. The term of life, fate, death, the hour of death, the predestined time of death, a stated time, delay, respite, period for the payment of a debt. اجل رسیده ajal rasīda, Overtaken by fate. اجل گرفته ajal-girifta, Seized by fate, on the point of death, in a situation that leaves no hopes of life, in the jaws of death.
subs: adj. = ajal-rasīda; and s. com. gend. One who is overtaken by fate; one whose hour of death is at hand; doomed person, &c.
wety: From Classical Persian اَجَل (ajal), from Arabic أَجَل (ʔajal).
